rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers in AGREE contracts are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Do not place assume/guarantee statements in implementation scopes; they must be in component type scopes.
Use only valid AADL feature names or declared auxiliary variables in AGREE contracts; avoid package-qualified identifiers not present in the model.
generic_rules: When AGREE reports type mismatches or unresolved references, trace the root cause to a missing declaration or incorrect identifier usage.
AGREE contracts must use valid scalar types: int, real, and bool; avoid referencing undefined or invalid type names.
Assignments in implementation scopes must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Avoid introducing new package dependencies or qualified names in AGREE annexes that are not present in the original model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes are for bindings.
Ensure all expressions in AGREE have consistent types; e.g., boolean expressions must evaluate to bool.
anti_patterns: Do not place assume or guarantee statements in component implementation scopes; they are only valid in component types.
Do not reference identifiers or types that are not declared or visible in the current AGREE annex or AADL model.
Do not attempt to fix type errors by adding architecture; instead, correct the root cause in the AGREE contract.
